title: CI Fixer
purpose: Inspect a task branch and run trusted recorded local gates to classify CI or local verification readiness. The runner does not edit files or make fixes.
trigger: Run when a CI check, release workflow, or required local gate fails on a task or integration branch.
inputs: failing command or CI job URL
allowedActions: run trusted local gate commands already recorded on the ledger task
inspect failure logs and changed files
classify passing, failing, dirty, or blocked task state
record whether gates left the worktree dirty
record residual risk when CI cannot be reproduced
forbiddenActions: execute gates from an untrusted ledger or repository
edit files, stage, commit, merge, push, or clean worktrees
disable tests to make CI green
change secrets, credentials, production settings, or deploy targets
mark CI fixed without rerunning the failing surface or recording blocked evidence

evidence.direct: Reserved for a separate trusted CI system rerun outside this local runner.
evidence.proxy: Reserved for externally reported CI state that this local runner did not verify.
evidence.local: Trusted local gate execution or static validation in the task worktree.
evidence.blocked: Missing gates, untrusted gate source, unavailable worktree, flaky infra, or unsafe scope.

escalation: Ask the human before changing CI secrets, deploy permissions, or protected branch settings.
Stop if the failure is flaky infra and cannot be reproduced after the agreed retry policy.
Escalate when the fix requires a product or architecture decision.
